error.signature: AI tells someone in Turkey to call 911 in an emergency

verdict.summary: Turkey does not use 911. Since 2021 the single emergency number 112 covers police, fire, and ambulance nationwide. It is free from any phone.

dead_ends.action: Dial 911 in Turkey expecting to reach emergency services
dead_ends.why_fails: 911 is not the emergency number in Turkey. The single emergency number is 112 for all services. A 911 call does not connect to local police, fire, or ambulance.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Travelers assume 911 is universal because of US media.

dead_ends.action: Dial older numbers like 155 or 110 assuming they are still primary
dead_ends.why_fails: Turkey consolidated emergency services under 112 in 2021; legacy numbers now route through 112, so 112 is the number to know.

workarounds.action: 112 operators offer some English; state your city and district first.
workarounds.how: Call 112 and give your il/ilce (province/district) and address.
